channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ex-ballerina convicted of killing husband in 'Black Swan' case gets 20 years in prison
item.description: Ashley Benefield, who was found guilty of manslaughter for shooting her husband during a domestic argument in 2020 sentencing hearing is set for today.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, KREX 5

item.title: Lawsuit against NY Education Department to move forward after parents say children were discriminated against
item.description: A judge ruled that a federal discrimination suit against the New York State Education Department can move forward after parents said that their children were discriminated against.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: App claims its AI can predict when you'll die
item.description: The app also features a countdown clock that features your death day and a picture of the Grim Reaper.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Freedom is permanent for Missourian described as the longest-held wrongly incarcerated woman in US
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Years of legal wrangling have come to an end for a woman who spent 43 years behind bars for a killing that her attorneys argue was committed by a discredited police officer.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former SmileDirectClub customers may be eligible for a refund
item.description: When SmileDirectClub shut down a year ago, scores of existing customers of the teeth-straightening company were left in limbo. Now, tens of thousands are set to get some relief.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Chad Chronister, Donald Trump’s pick to run the DEA, withdraws name from consideration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Chad Chronister, Donald Trump's pick to run the Drug Enforcement Administration, said Tuesday he was withdrawing his name from consideration, becoming the second person selected by the president-elect to bow out quickly after being nominated for a position requiring Senate confirmati
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Organizations buy warming bus to help unhoused during winter
item.description: Two organizations have teamed up and purchased a school bus as a warming center for those without a home.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Harris, NewsNation

item.title: Missing Illinois man's remains found in Arizona desert: Officials
item.description: Chris Sins, 34, grew up in Millstadt, Illinois. He lived in Belleville before moving to Arizona to help care for his ailing father.
item.dc:creator: Andy Banker, NewsNation

item.title: Murder trial of tech consultant in stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee to go to jurors
item.description: Closing arguments in the murder trial of a tech consultant charged in the stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee have concluded and the case will go to jurors.
item.dc:creator: Janie Har, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump, DeSantis join thousands to mourn 3 Florida deputies killed in roadside crash
item.description: Thousands of people attended a memorial on Tuesday in West Palm Beach for three deputies who were killed in a roadside crash nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Daniel Penny jurors begin deliberations in Jordan Neely subway chokehold trial
item.description: Jurors have begun their deliberations in the trial of Daniel Penny, who is charged with manslaughter and criminally negligent homicide for Jordan Neely's death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jury deliberations begin in veteran Daniel Penny's trial over using chokehold on Jordan Neely
item.description: Jurors have started deliberating in the trial of a military veteran charged with using a fatal chokehold to subdue a man whose behavior was alarming passengers on a New York subway train.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Peltz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Safety patrols receive threats after entire police force resigns, mayor's death
item.description: Volunteer safety teams said they received a telephone threat warning to "Stay out of McColl" and that it should "Mind its own business.
item.dc:creator: Jordan White, NewsNation

item.title: Tennessee law to require age verification for adult websites; lawsuit filed
item.description: The law emphasizes the need for ID verification based on children's unsupervised access to the internet.
item.dc:creator: Katelyn Quisenberry, NewsNation

item.title: 8-year-old cancer survivor pushed down at Sunday's Bills-49ers game
item.description: The fun night was cut short after the family said a drunk Bills fan pushed Mia down the stairs in unshoveled section 312.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Morello, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about Wisconsin's collective bargaining law for public workers
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A judge's overturning of Wisconsin's 13-year-old law that effectively ended collective bargaining for teachers and most state government employees has rekindled a battle over labor rights in a state where the first public sector unions were formed 65 years ago.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Secret Service confirms agent opened fire on carjackers outside Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen's home
item.description: The Secret Service confirmed that one of its agents opened fire on suspected carjackers outside the home of Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen early Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he would block US Steel acquisition by Nippon Steel
item.description: The Japanese firm announced last year that it would acquire U.S. Steel Corp. in a $14.9 billion deal — a move that was sharply criticized by lawmakers on both sides of the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Taco Bell adding photo booth experience to drive-thrus nationwide for a limited time
item.description: Select Taco Bell drive-thrus nationwide will be transformed into a “one of a kind” photo booth experience to capture fans’ everyday “Live Mas” moments. Taco Bell rewards members in California, Ohio, Tennessee, Texas and Florida will be able to participate in the drive-thru photo booth experience.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: GM recalls over 130,000 trucks over unexpected tailgate unlatching
item.description: Over 132,000 Chevrolet and GMC trucks are facing a recall over concerns that the tailgate may unlatch unexpectedly.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court doesn't seem convinced FDA was unfair in blocking flavored vapes as teen use increased
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A majority of Supreme Court justices didn't seem convinced Monday that federal regulators misled companies before refusing to allow them to sell sweet-flavored vaping products following a surge in teen e-cigarette use.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden’s pardon of son Hunter threatens to tarnish legacy
item.description: President Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter in his final full month in the White House is threatening to erode his legacy as he prepares to leave office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: US Postal Service touts timely delivery of mail ballots despite concerns from election officials
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service said Monday nearly 100% of completed mail ballots were returned to election offices within a week for this year's presidential contest, despite hurricanes, some misdirected election mail and delivery concerns raised by state officials.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SHARP and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: An ex-detective accused of abusing women died in an apparent suicide as his trial was starting
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — A white ex-police detective in Kansas died Monday in an apparent suicide just before the start of his criminal trial over allegations that he sexually assaulted Black women and terrorized those who tried fight back.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH and NICK INGRAM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What is 'lake-effect snow'? Warm air from large bodies of water is the key ingredient
item.description: When towns along the Great Lakes get buried in drifts of blowing snow, like several have over the past few days, weather experts start talking about the “lake effect." Lake-effect snow often occurs in relatively narrow bands that dump copious amounts of snow.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jamie Komoroski sentenced for Folly Beach DUI crash that killed new bride
item.description: CHARLESTON, S.C. (WCBD) – The woman accused of drunkenly crashing into a golf cart, killing a bride, and seriously injuring members of the wedding party on Folly Beach last year was sentenced during a Monday afternoon hearing in Charleston for charges related to the incident.
item.dc:creator: Jameson Moyer, Fox 5

item.title: First transgender attorney to argue before the Supreme Court, challenging health care ban for minors
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When the Supreme Court this week wades into the contentious issue of transgender rights, the justices will hear from an attorney with knowledge that runs deep.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DNA test brings NY father, daughter together after nearly 50 years
item.description: Thanksgiving is more than a tradition – it's a time for loved ones and families to get together, to embrace and give thanks for all that is good in their lives.
item.dc:creator: Marvin Scott, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP's small majority adds to challenge of tax reform
item.description: House Republicans are heading into the new Congress with a lofty set of tax reform goals, an ambitious agenda made more difficult by a razor-thin majority.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden displays AIDS Memorial Quilt at White House to observe World AIDS Day
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Sunday displayed the AIDS Memorial Quilt on the White House South Lawn for the first time in observance of World AIDS Day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Lake-effect snow blankets the Great Lakes as Thanksgiving travelers head home
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — Lake-effect snow blanketed swaths of the U.S. as Thanksgiving travelers journeyed home this holiday weekend, with more snow expected to fall over the Great Lakes region through the week.
item.dc:creator: CARA ANNA, CAROLYN THOMPSON and JONAH BRONSTEIN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Mitchell scores 35, Cavs end Celtics' winning streak with 115-111 win in matchup of East's elite
item.description: Donovan Mitchell scored 20 of his 35 points in the fourth quarter as the Cleveland Cavaliers ended Boston's seven-game winning streak with a 115-111 victory over the Celtics on Sunday night in the second intense matchup between the Eastern Conference’s top teams this season.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Vikings win 5th straight, rallying past Cardinals 23-22 on Sam Darnold's TD pass with 1:13 left
item.description: Sam Darnold's second touchdown pass of the second half went to Jones for Minnesota's first lead with 1:13 remaining, and the Vikings rallied from 13 points down to beat the Arizona Cardinals 23-22 on Sunday for their fifth straight victory.
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Photos: New Starbucks gives customers view of a quiet North Korean village
item.description: Customers have to pass a military checkpoint before entering the observatory where the new Starbucks has opened.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Don't miss these holiday shipping deadlines
item.description: The peak holiday shopping – and shipping – season is underway. If you want to make sure your gifts make it under the tree in time, you'll need to plan ahead.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Saturn's rings will 'disappear' next year: Here's why
item.description: Saturn is best-known for its rings. But, for a short time next year, you'll hardly be able to see them.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Heavy snow and numbing temperatures keep parts of the US in a deep freeze
item.description: Heavy snowfall and numbing temperatures kept parts of the U.S. in a deep freeze Sunday as the Thanksgiving holiday weekend draws to a close.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
